feed.title: Prakrut Chaubal
feed.subtitle: Personal webpage and blog of Prakrut Chaubal, a cosmologist with the South Pole Telescope.

entry.title: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo
entry.summary: HMC is a popular sampler for estimating the posterior densities in Bayesian analysis. I have resisted learning about it since long, disparaging it as yet another random walk algorithm. The ignorance finally started hurting me so here I am trying to understand it. I am writing about it assuming undergrad level physics knowledge so I will skip all the trivialities of Legendre transforms. The eq...

entry.title: Neural ODE solver
entry.summary: Introduction The Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) radiation is the left over of the Big Bang that provides us the earliest snapshot of the Universe. This radiation is isotropic with small random fluctuations. Despite the random nature of these perturbations, our physical models allow us to predict the some statistical propeties of these fluctuations such as the power spectrum. This post is all...

entry.title: How good is GPT for Time Series analysis?

link.title: How good is GPT for Time Series analysis?

entry.summary: tldr; Testing two hypothesis, GPT models are great at learning large scale correlations with timeseries GPT models able to store some information about a ‘metastate’ which helps it talk about some context Unless you are living under a rock, you should now be fed up of people telling you how is awesome is the GPT architechture for large language models. GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 models have alre...

entry.title: Butterfly connection
entry.summary: Introduction Can ML learn an optimized algorithm such as Fast Fourier Transform ? Like everyone today, I too have been following the progress with large language models (LLMs) and I came across a blog post1 about Hyena models. While I confess that I am not at all interested in the language part of LLM, I usually keep up wiht these works only to see what I could use in my work, which is mostly q...

entry.title: Covariance Matrix estimation
entry.summary: I found myself wondering about a better way to estimate the covariance matrix for CMB. The code for this is available on Github: Github repo The Problem The South Pole Telescope observes the sky multiple times and adds up these maps into partial depth coadds. Each such coadd has around 2-5 individual sky observations. We then create the cross correlate these maps with other partial depth coadds...
